story:
I got this diary for my birthday in 2015, right after I moved, and I was excited to write about soccer and school and everything. Almost immediately, drama: Oliver told me Henry like-likes me. I barely knew him, but everyone liked him, and Mia said I should say something fast. I didn’t. Then I found out he was dating Emma, which is when I realized I actually did like him. They broke up later, but I still couldn’t tell if he liked me. I tried to move on—Evan was a jerk, Levi was annoying—so I stayed stuck on Henry. In January 2016 I finally tried to get closure. I asked Eve to ask him if he liked me, and he looked disgusted. I wanted to disappear.

I lost the diary for a while and found it again in October 2017, right before Halloween. I had a new friend, Hannah, from Mrs. Hudson’s class. We dressed up as minions and went trick-or-treating in her neighborhood—big candy bars, even hot cocoa at one house. I wished we lived closer because she was so funny and smart, and I hoped I was her best friend like she was mine. On the first night of Chanukah, my family went to her house; her mom made dinner, we lit the menorah, and we exchanged gifts that showed we really knew each other: I gave her a Minecraft LEGO set; she gave me a Michael’s gift card. It felt like best-friend energy.

Then, early 2018, Hannah got a crush on Nick. Suddenly it was all Nick, all the time. Then they started dating, and she saw him most weekends. I wanted to be happy for her, but I missed how we used to laugh about videos and talk Minecraft. Things got worse. She told me she’d try to make my bat mitzvah but then posted a picture with Nick the same day. Later I learned I wasn’t even invited to hers. She left my texts on read. I didn’t know what I’d done wrong, only that my best friend didn’t seem to want me anymore.

In January 2019 my math teacher changed our seats and I ended up next to Henry. We hadn’t talked in forever, but he asked how I’d been, and we chatted before class most days. Chelsea said that meant he liked me, which made my old crush flare up again, even though I was trying not to care. Then I found out he was dating Alexa from chorus. Weirdly, I was happy for them. Alexa was nice—she sounded amazing at Glee Day, complimented me back when I complimented her, and even said she liked my outfit once. I overheard she was moving after the school year. I barely knew her, but I was sad to lose someone that kind.

By June 2020, with extra time and a lot on my mind, I reread everything. It made me think about how I felt about Hannah and how I noticed Alexa. I was scared to say it out loud in case I was wrong or attention-seeking, even though I knew my friends and parents would support me. But I wanted to be honest. I decided to start by telling my friends: I’m bi. Maybe I’ll start a new, more grown-up journal. Maybe this isn’t the end. But it feels like a beginning.
